articles.title: A Volcanic Medley Near Mammoth Lakes
articles.summary: A massive, old caldera and more recently formed craters shape the landscape in the eastern Sierra Nevada.

articles.title: Celestial Wonders in Leo
articles.summary: Leo is a prominent sight for stargazers in April. Its famous sickle, punctuated by the bright star Regulus, draws many a beginning stargazer’s eyes, inviting deeper looks into some of Leo’s celestial delights, including a great double star and a famous galactic trio. Leo’s distinctive forward sickle, or “reverse question mark,” is easy to spot […]

articles.title: Stunning Images, A Chat with Friends — Another Great Day for Artemis II
articles.summary: The Artemis II crew is on their way back to Earth after swinging around the Moon yesterday. Stunning images of both the Moon and the Earth have been downlinked already […]

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
articles.summary: The first flyby images of the Moon captured by NASA’s Artemis II astronauts during their historic test flight reveal some regions no human has seen, including a rare in-space solar eclipse. Released Tuesday, astronauts captured the images April 6 during the mission’s seven-hour flyby of the lunar far side, showing humanity’s return to the Moon’s […]

articles.title: Artemis II Flies Around the Moon for All
articles.summary: On April 6th, 2026, humans visited the Moon for the first time in 54 years. Artemis II has completed its lunar flyby, achieving the literal and figurative apex of the mission as four people beheld parts of the surface never seen with human eyes.

articles.title: Hungary taps Northrop Grumman for first national geostationary communications satellite
articles.summary:
Hungary’s defense and space tech contractor 4iG also announced deals with L3Harris and Apex for defense systems and satellite manufacturing

articles.title: Earthset
articles.summary: The Artemis II crew captured this view of Earth setting on April 6, 2026, as they flew around the Moon. As the astronauts flew over the Moon’s far side, the crew photographed and described terrain features including impact craters, ancient lava flows, and surface cracks and ridges formed as the Moon slowly evolved over time. […]

articles.summary: Digital creators and social media users are invited to register to attend the rollout of the third SLS (Space Launch System) core stage as it is prepped to head from NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility to NASA’s Kennedy Space Center. articles.title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
articles.summary: Media will have an opportunity at 9:45 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, to speak with NASA’s Artemis II crew members as they continue their journey toward Earth during their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will take place with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA […]

articles.title: The Artemis II mission sends back stunning images of the far side of the Moon
articles.summary: The Moon, the Earth, and the Sun; oh what fun!
